segments.text:
LYSOSOME
Function: degradation of macromolecules
Acidic environment
Discovered by Christian de Duve (1974 Nobel Prize)

SOLUBLE LYSOSOMAL ENZYMES
Contain recognition sequence for addition of phosphate to mannose of oligosaccharide
Creates mannose-6-phosphate (M6P) signal
Phosphate added in cis-Golgi

PATHWAY TO LYSOSOME
M6P receptor binds to M6P in TGN
Vesicle fuses with late endosome
Protein separates from receptor (low pH in LE)
Phosphate removed in LE
Transport to lysosome

DEGRADATION OF TRANSMEMBRANE PROTEINS
Problem – digestive enzymes in lumen of lysosome but part of transmembrane protein is cytosolic
Multivesicular bodies form so proteins are within lumen after fusion

FORMATION OF MULTIVESICULAR BODIES
Complex of ESCRT (endosomal sorting complexes required for transport) proteins

FORMATION OF MULTIVESICULAR BODIES (BETTER DETAIL)
See ESCRT video

AUTOPHAGY
Yoshinori Ohsumi – 2016 Nobel Prize
Random mutagenesis of yeast cells
Screen for autophagy defects
Characterized “autophagy-related genes” (Atg) using complementation rescue experiments

MAIN STEPS IN AUTOPHAGY
1 – Phagophore assembly site (PAS) forms
Components to be degraded recruited through selective autophagy receptors (SARs)
2 – PAS recruits Atg9 and COPII vesicles

MAIN STEPS IN AUTOPHAGY
3 – Atg8 needed to add more vesicles
4,5,6 – Autophagosome seals, fuses, and degraded
